  "findings": [
    "The tension in each sling leg (3,535.5 lb) exceeds the tagged Working Load Limit (3,000 lb) of the individual legs."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not proceed with the lift using this configuration.",
    "Select slings with a higher Working Load Limit (at least 4,000 lb vertical rating) or increase the horizontal sling angle to reduce leg tension."
  ],
  "rationale": "The load is 5,000 lb, shared equally by two legs, resulting in a vertical load of 2,500 lb per leg. At a 45-degree horizontal sling angle, the tension in each leg is calculated as 2,500 lb / sin(45°) = 3,535.5 lb. Since this tension exceeds the tagged WLL of 3,000 lb per leg, the configuration is unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
